report_name: Ransomware group leaks data from contractor of India's largest nuclear plant

threat_actors.name: World Leaks
threat_actors.description: A ransomware group, believed to be a rebrand of Hunters International, that claimed responsibility for a data breach at Reliance Group, a contractor for India's Kudankulam Nuclear Power Plant. The group leaked 14.3 GB of data on the dark web as part of a double extortion scheme.

threat_actors.name: Hunters International
threat_actors.description: A ransomware group that 'World Leaks' is suspected to be a rebrand of.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Cloud Storage Object
attack_patterns.description: The attack targeted data at rest in a cloud/hosted environment, specifically a server hosted by a third-party data center, from which 14.3 GB of files were stolen.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor exfiltrated approximately 14.3 GB of data by likely compressing it into archives and transferring it to an actor-controlled server.

attack_patterns.name: Supply Chain Compromise
attack_patterns.description: The attack was a classic supply chain attack targeting a contractor (Reliance Group) to acquire sensitive information about the primary target (Kudankulam Nuclear Power Plant).

identities.name: Reliance Group
identities.description: A major engineering contractor for India's Kudankulam Nuclear Power Plant who was the direct victim of the data breach. The company confirmed a 'partial breach' on a server hosted by a third-party data center.

identities.name: Kudankulam Nuclear Power Plant (KKNPP)
identities.description: India's largest nuclear power plant. While not directly breached, sensitive engineering and logistical data related to its construction and support systems were leaked from its contractor, Reliance Group.

identities.name: Yotta
identities.description: A third-party data center provider that hosted the server for Reliance Group from which data was breached.

identities.name: Nuclear Power Corporation of India Limited (NPCIL)
identities.description: India's nuclear operator, which runs the Kudankulam Nuclear Power Plant. They stated that no sensitive nuclear security systems were compromised in the incident.

infrastructure.name: World Leaks Dark Web Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web site used by the World Leaks ransomware group to publish the 14.3 GB of data stolen from Reliance Group.

indicators.name: Anomalous Large Cloud Egress Traffic
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Large Egress Traffic from Cloud Storage
id: 9c7b9e8f-1d2a-4b3c-8a9b-0c1d2e3f4a5b
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects anomalous, large-scale data transfers from cloud storage buckets or hosted servers to unknown external IP addresses. This is a behavioral hint from the report on the World Leaks breach.
references:
  - https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/7/16/data-breach-reportedly-targets-indias-kudankulam-nuclear-power-plant
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-17
tags:
  - attack.t1567
  - attack.exfiltration
level: medium
logsource:
  category: firewall
detection:
  selection:
    action: 'allow'
    # This is a conceptual rule. Thresholds for 'bytes_out' need to be established based on baseline traffic.
    # Example: Look for outbound traffic from a data center IP range exceeding a high threshold (e.g., 10GB) in a short time.
    bytes_out|gt: 10000000000 # 10 GB
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects anomalous, large-scale data transfers from cloud buckets or hosted servers to unknown external IPs, as suggested in the article's hunting hints.

indicators.name: Suspicious Mass S3 Object Retrieval
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Mass S3 Object Retrieval
id: 8d6a5b4e-2c3b-4a1d-9b8a-1d2e3f4a5b6c
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects a high volume of GetObject API calls from a single user or source IP, which could indicate mass data download from an S3 bucket. This is based on hunting hints from the World Leaks breach report.
references:
  - https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/7/16/data-breach-reportedly-targets-indias-kudankulam-nuclear-power-plant
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-17
tags:
  - attack.t1530
  - attack.collection
level: medium
logsource:
  product: aws
  service: cloudtrail
detection:
  selection:
    eventSource: 's3.amazonaws.com'
    eventName: 'GetObject'
  condition: selection | count(user) by sourceIPAddress > 1000
indicators.description: Detects suspicious API calls related to mass data access from cloud storage, such as a high volume of 'GetObject' calls, as suggested in the article's hunting hints.

relationships.target_name: World Leaks Dark Web Leak Site
Nuclear Power Corporation of India Limited (NPCIL)
